name: Facebook Ads Library MCP Server
what_it_does: Facebook Ads Library MCP server enabling AI agents to search and analyze the Facebook Ads Library — querying active and inactive ad campaigns across Meta platforms (Facebook, Instagram), retrieving ad creative details, analyzing competitor advertising strategies, and integrating Meta's public ad transparency data into agent-driven competitive intelligence and market research workflows.
use_cases: Researching competitor ad strategies from competitive intelligence agents
Analyzing ad creative and messaging trends from marketing research agents
Monitoring political and issue advertising from transparency research agents
Tracking brand advertising activity from brand monitoring agents
Building ad intelligence reports from marketing analytics agents
Identifying advertising patterns and seasonal trends from market research agents
not_for: Managing or placing Facebook ads (Library is read-only transparency tool)
Teams needing actual ad performance data (Library shows ads, not metrics)
Teams using this to scrape at scale (Meta rate-limits and ToS applies)
best_when: An agent needs to research advertising strategies via Meta's public ad transparency tool — for competitive intelligence, market research, or monitoring advertising trends.
avoid_when: You need ad performance metrics, conversion data, or want to manage campaigns — use Meta Business API instead.

auth.notes: Facebook Graph API access token required. App access token (not user token) works for public Ad Library data. Facebook developer app needed. Ad Library API access requires separate verification.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Facebook Ad Library API: free but rate-limited
pricing.paid_tiers: Meta Marketing API: rate limits increase with Meta Business verification
pricing.notes: Ad Library API access is free but requires Facebook developer account and verification. Rate limits apply. Community MCP is free.

performance.rate_limits: Meta Graph API: rate limits vary by app type and verification status

known_agent_gotchas: Facebook Ad Library API requires separate verification beyond basic developer account
Access token type matters: app access token (APPID|APPSECRET) vs user token
Ad Library API only shows paid ads — organic posts not included
Political/issue ads have additional data; commercial ads have limited info
Pagination uses cursor-based approach with limited total result sets
Community MCP — Meta Graph API version compatibility must be maintained
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. App access token (contains app secret). GDPR. Community MCP. Store access token securely — use app-level token not user token for public data.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. App access token — store securely (contains app secret). GDPR. Community MCP.
